_meta.description: Reference bank of REAL HKDSE English Paper 2 Writing questions (2012-2024)
_meta.purpose: Few-shot examples for AI generation of structurally equivalent prompts with original topics

prompts.topic: Feature article about yourself as a news reporter
prompts.context: You are a famous news reporter for Hong Kong News. Your old secondary school has asked you to write a feature article about yourself for the next edition of the school magazine.
prompts.task: Use the following three headings to write the article. Add one heading of your own: The best part of my job, The biggest challenge I have ever faced in my job, My school days, [One heading of your own]

prompts.topic: Virtual sports vs real sports
prompts.context: You have been following an online debate in Health and Fitness eMagazine about the value of doing virtual sports (such as those played on a Wii) versus real sports.
prompts.task: Write your view on the topic and post it on the online forum.

prompts.topic: Educational benefits of using drama in English lessons
prompts.context: You have been following an online discussion about the educational benefits of using drama in English lessons.
prompts.task: Explain the educational benefits (or lack thereof) of using drama in lessons and share your experience of learning through drama.

prompts.topic: First performance at a music festival
prompts.context: You are the lead singer in a band. Last week you held your first performance at Victoria Park.
prompts.task: Write on your blog describing the performance, including how you felt, the atmosphere, and the things you could have done better.

prompts.topic: Recruiting school debating club members
prompts.context: You are the chairperson of your school's debating club. You have been asked to give a speech in the school assembly to recruit new members.
prompts.task: Explain the activities the debating club organizes, the benefits of being in the club, and the importance in life of being a good debater.

prompts.topic: Cosmetic surgery and obsession with physical beauty
prompts.context: There has been much discussion in the media about young people's obsession with physical beauty and the increasing popularity of cosmetic surgery.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor of the South China Morning Post expressing your views on cosmetic surgery and the obsession with physical beauty.

prompts.topic: Poor brother driving an expensive sports car
prompts.context: Your brother has always been poor at managing his finances. One day you see him driving an expensive sports car.
prompts.task: Write a short story explaining how you found out the truth about the sports car.

prompts.topic: Campaign about recruiting criminals or drug addicts back into the workforce
prompts.context: The government has launched a campaign to recruit criminals and drug addicts back into the workforce. Your company is considering joining this scheme.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to your employer outlining the new project, explaining the benefits, and persuading the business to join the scheme.

prompts.topic: How to write a good short story
prompts.context: A famous author recently gave a talk at your school about what makes a good short story. Your school magazine editor has asked you to write about it.
prompts.task: Write an article for the school magazine explaining why you found the talk interesting and what you learned about writing short stories.

prompts.topic: Photo captions about a school event
prompts.context: Your school is putting together a photo album of the school's annual Open Day. You have been asked to write captions for four photos.
prompts.task: Write four photo captions describing different aspects of the Open Day. Each caption should be about 50 words.

prompts.topic: Producing a programme about a local Hong Kong sporting event
prompts.context: Your school is producing a programme about a local Hong Kong sporting event. As a member of the production team, you need to email your teacher with your ideas.
prompts.task: Suggest a sporting event to feature, explain what would be included in the programme, and outline how to make the programme interesting to schoolmates.

prompts.topic: Betraying best friend (referencing Julius Caesar)
prompts.context: You are reading Julius Caesar by William Shakespeare for drama class. In the play, Brutus betrays his friend Caesar. Your homework is to write a diary entry about a situation in which you betrayed someone or were betrayed.
prompts.task: Write a diary entry describing an instance of betrayal, making reference to the plot of Julius Caesar.

prompts.topic: Private school bus safety concerns
prompts.context: There have been reports about private school bus service operators not following proper safety procedures. You are concerned about the safety of younger students.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the Bus Operators Association expressing your concerns and giving three recommendations for how private school bus services can be improved.

prompts.topic: People making unreasonable complaints
prompts.context: Some people in Hong Kong frequently make unreasonable complaints about various issues, taking advantage of the city's complaint system.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor describing a situation where some people complained about something, outline their point of view, and indicate why their complaints are unreasonable.

prompts.topic: Many people leaving the office late
prompts.context: At your workplace, many employees are staying in the office late into the evening. Your manager has asked you to write an article for the company newsletter about this issue.
prompts.task: Describe the situation, discuss the negative effects of working late, and give two suggestions to improve the situation.

prompts.topic: Popularity of Asian stars in Western countries
prompts.context: Asian pop culture, including K-pop and J-pop, has been gaining popularity in Western countries. Your school newspaper editor wants an article on this trend.
prompts.task: Write an article for the school newspaper explaining the reasons for the rise of Asian stars' popularity in Western countries.

prompts.topic: Making good use of time
prompts.context: You have won the Best All-round Student Award at your school. You are giving a speech to your fellow students to share the secret of success. You are inspired by a popular song about making every moment count.
prompts.task: Write a speech explaining how time can slip away if you don't make good use of it and how to make good use of time. Refer to the song that inspires you.
taskRequirements: Need to refer to the song provided

prompts.topic: Vandals hit museum
prompts.context: You are entering the Imaginative Story Competition and have been given the following situation: Vandals have broken into a museum and damaged several priceless masterpieces.
prompts.task: Write a short story explaining why the masterpieces were damaged and what happened as a result.

prompts.topic: Local history newsletter article
prompts.context: Your school is publishing a local history newsletter. You have been asked to contribute a short article about a historical building or area in your district.
prompts.task: Write a short article for the local history newsletter describing a historical building or area in your district, its history, and its significance. Use the headings provided.

prompts.topic: Requesting donation of old equipment from a fitness centre
prompts.context: Your school is setting up a new fitness room and needs equipment. A local fitness centre is upgrading its facilities and may be willing to donate its old equipment.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the fitness centre manager describing how the donation could benefit both the school and the fitness centre.

prompts.topic: Poor behaviour during a drama performance
prompts.context: During a drama performance at school, some audience members behaved poorly. As the chairperson of the Drama Club, you need to address this.
prompts.task: Write a formal email to Drama Club members describing the complaint received, explaining what behaviour is prohibited and encouraged, and giving reasons.

prompts.topic: Filming movies in the city centre
prompts.context: Filming of movies in Hong Kong's city centre has been causing disruption to residents and businesses. There is debate about whether permits should be restricted.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor of a local newspaper giving your opinion on whether filming should be restricted in the city centre and giving three reasons to support your view.

prompts.topic: Limited access to school lockers
prompts.context: Your school has limited the hours during which students can access their lockers. As the chairperson of the Student Union, you disagree with this policy.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the school Principal persuading her to change the policy on lockers and allow students more access.

prompts.topic: Summer job experiences as a costumed character performer
prompts.context: You spent your summer working as a costumed character performer at a theme park in Hong Kong. A local newspaper has asked you to write about your experience.
prompts.task: Write a newspaper article describing your typical working day, the good things and bad things about the job.

prompts.topic: Stand-up comedy show
prompts.context: You recently attended a stand-up comedy show. Your school magazine editor has asked you to write about the experience.
prompts.task: Write an article for the school magazine describing the show, discussing the challenges performers might face, and explaining how students can benefit from watching stand-up comedy.

prompts.topic: Hong Kong Schools Poetry Festival
prompts.context: You participated in the Hong Kong Schools Poetry Festival. Your school magazine editor has asked you to write about your experience.
prompts.task: Write a feature article for the school magazine describing how you prepared for the competition and what you learned from this experience.

prompts.topic: Roommate's sudden departure
prompts.context: You live in a school hostel. One day your roommate suddenly leaves.
prompts.task: Write a short story describing the events that led to your roommate's sudden departure and explain why your roommate suddenly left.

prompts.topic: Complaint about poor customer service
prompts.context: You recently purchased a pair of headphones from an electronics store. The headphones stopped working after only two weeks. When you tried to return them, the store refused to give you a refund.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter of complaint to the customer service manager. Describe the problem, explain why you are dissatisfied, and state what you expect the company to do.

prompts.topic: Disappointing PE lessons
prompts.context: Many students in your school are unhappy with the current PE lessons. You have been asked to write to the Principal about this.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the Principal explaining why classmates are unhappy about existing PE lessons, suggesting activities that could be introduced, and explaining why students would prefer them.

prompts.topic: Elderly realizing their dreams
prompts.context: A community centre is running a programme that helps elderly people realize their dreams. Write a story inspired by this programme.
prompts.task: Write a short story about elderly people realizing their dreams, describing the challenges they face and the joy of achievement.

prompts.topic: Sending children abroad for schooling
prompts.context: Many Hong Kong parents are sending their children to other countries to complete their schooling. Your school magazine is exploring this phenomenon.
prompts.task: Write an article for the school magazine examining two reasons for this phenomenon and discussing the impact on local schools and on children who leave.

prompts.topic: Land use of undeveloped harbour front area
prompts.context: The government is considering how to use a large undeveloped area along the harbour front. There is debate about whether it should be left open for public use or developed.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor stating your opinion either for or against leaving the area open for public use and give three reasons to support your view.

prompts.topic: Handling complaints about the company
prompts.context: Your company has received several complaints from customers recently. Your manager has asked you to look into the situation.
prompts.task: Write an email to the manager summarizing the details of a recent complaint and suggesting three possible actions the company could take.

prompts.topic: Fewer people going to cinemas than before
prompts.context: The number of people going to cinemas has been declining. A report is needed to understand this trend.
prompts.task: Write a report examining the reasons for the decline in cinema attendance and suggesting measures to address the issue.

prompts.topic: Language arts festival activities
prompts.context: The Central Library is organizing a Language Arts Festival. Your class has been invited to suggest activities for the festival.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the Central Library suggesting two interesting activities that would increase students' interest in learning English and explaining how these activities would achieve this goal.

prompts.topic: Suggesting an author for a school visit
prompts.context: Your school is planning to invite an author to speak to students. You have been asked to recommend a suitable author.
prompts.task: Write a formal email to the teacher explaining why you have selected a particular author and suggesting activities the author can either lead or help with during the visit.

prompts.topic: Speech about the importance of community service
prompts.context: Your school is launching a community service programme. As the head prefect, you have been asked to give a speech during morning assembly to encourage students to participate.
prompts.task: Write a speech explaining the importance of community service, what activities will be available, and how students will benefit from participating.

prompts.topic: Using school sports facilities after hours
prompts.context: There is debate about whether the public should be allowed to use school sports facilities after school hours.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor of a local newspaper stating your opinion on allowing the public to use school sports facilities and give three reasons.

prompts.topic: Studying at HKAPA
prompts.context: You want to study at the Hong Kong Academy for Performing Arts (HKAPA) but your parents are not supportive. You need to convince them.
prompts.task: Write a personal email to your parents to persuade them to support your decision to study at HKAPA instead of another institution.

prompts.topic: Parents installing monitoring apps on children's phones
prompts.context: Some parents install apps on their children's mobile phones to monitor their activities. There is debate about whether this is acceptable.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor stating whether you agree with parents installing monitoring apps and give three reasons.

prompts.topic: Hong Kong's disappearing street life
prompts.context: Hong Kong's unique street life and local culture are fast disappearing due to urban development.
prompts.task: Write an essay focusing on one aspect of local street life or culture that is fast disappearing and suggest why it is worth preserving.

prompts.topic: Graduates finding jobs in other Asian cities
prompts.context: Many Hong Kong graduates are considering finding jobs in other cities in Asia such as Singapore, Shanghai, and Tokyo.
prompts.task: Write an essay discussing your views on Hong Kong graduates seeking employment in other Asian cities and give three reasons.

prompts.topic: Proposing a Youth Festival to showcase youth trends
prompts.context: The Hong Kong Youth Council is considering organizing a Youth Festival to showcase youth trends and culture.
prompts.task: Write a proposal suggesting one or two youth trends to feature and ways to present them at the festival.

prompts.topic: Fear and how to cope with challenges
prompts.context: You have been studying poems and songs about fear. You are now writing an essay reflecting on the theme.
prompts.task: Write an essay explaining how important it is to acknowledge the challenges we face and find ways to cope with them. Use relevant personal examples and refer to the quote provided.

prompts.topic: Revenge is sweet
prompts.context: The theme for this year's short story competition is Revenge is Sweet.
prompts.task: Write a short story explaining why the protagonist decided to take revenge and what happened as a result.

prompts.topic: Letter to the Principal about a proposed school trip
prompts.context: Your class would like to organize a trip to the Hong Kong Science Museum. You need to write to the Principal to seek approval.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the Principal proposing the trip, explaining its educational value, and outlining the itinerary and costs involved.

prompts.topic: Hong Kong Marathon
prompts.context: You recently participated in the Hong Kong Marathon. The marathon's official website is featuring blog posts from participants.
prompts.task: Write a blog entry for the Hong Kong Marathon website describing your experience and encouraging other young people to take part in the next marathon.

prompts.topic: Wildlife
prompts.context: Write a story for a creative writing competition.
prompts.task: Write a short story describing the events leading to a lion climbing up a kitchen stove.

prompts.topic: Romantic love and happy marriage
prompts.context: You have been studying poems and songs about love. Your teacher has asked you to write an essay on the relationship between romantic love and marriage.
prompts.task: Write an essay defining romantic love and happy marriage, and explaining why romantic love is or is not necessary for a happy marriage.

prompts.topic: Protecting intangible cultural heritage
prompts.context: Hong Kong's intangible cultural heritage, such as traditional egg tarts and mahjong, is at risk of disappearing. There is debate about what should be protected.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor explaining reasons why we should protect intangible cultural heritage and suggesting ways to do so.

prompts.topic: Watching TV makes us smarter
prompts.context: You are participating in a school debate on the motion Watching TV Makes Us Smarter.
prompts.task: Write a debate speech arguing either for or against the motion. Give three reasons to support your position.

prompts.topic: Hong Kong NEETs (Not in Education, Employment, or Training)
prompts.context: The number of young people in Hong Kong who are Not in Education, Employment, or Training (NEETs) is rising.
prompts.task: Write a report explaining why the number of NEETs in Hong Kong is rising, suggesting what can be done to help these youths, and giving reasons to support your suggestions.

prompts.topic: Dancers are athletes
prompts.context: There is debate about whether dancers should be considered athletes. Your school magazine is running a feature on this topic.
prompts.task: Write a school magazine article about the physical strength and skills required as a member of a dancing team, and the self-discipline and training involved.

prompts.topic: Graduates being less hard-working
prompts.context: There have been comments in the media suggesting that recent graduates are less hard-working and less willing to face challenges.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor disagreeing with this view and giving three reasons and/or examples to support your position.

prompts.topic: Letter to a friend about a summer job opportunity
prompts.context: You have learned about a summer job opportunity at a local bookstore. You want to recommend it to your friend who is looking for summer work.
prompts.task: Write a letter to your friend describing the job opportunity, explaining why it would be suitable for them, and encouraging them to apply.

prompts.topic: Hong Kong Marathon
prompts.context: You are writing a blog for the Hong Kong Marathon website.
prompts.task: Write a blog entry encouraging other young people to take part in the next marathon. Share your experience and the benefits of participating.

prompts.topic: Showing video-recordings of live drama performances
prompts.context: A debate has arisen about whether theatre groups should show video-recordings of live drama performances to attract more audiences.
prompts.task: Write a feature article for a magazine explaining why video-recordings are or are not a good way to attract more people to watch stage plays.

prompts.topic: Parental intervention in choosing a career
prompts.context: You receive a letter from a teenager whose parents want to choose their career path. You need to write a reply for a teen magazine advice column.
prompts.task: Write a letter of advice for a teen magazine responding to a teenager whose parents are intervening in their career choice.

prompts.topic: Reporting students' class position should be abolished
prompts.context: Your school is debating the motion The policy of reporting students class position should be abolished.
prompts.task: Write a debate speech arguing either for or against the motion.

prompts.topic: Complaints about work-related emails out of office hours
prompts.context: Employees in your company have complained about receiving work-related emails and text messages outside of office hours. As the HR manager, you need to address this.
prompts.task: Write a letter to staff addressing the complaints about out-of-hours communications and explaining the company's position.

prompts.topic: Trend of athleisure
prompts.context: The trend of athleisure wearing sportswear as everyday fashion has become increasingly popular. Your local newspaper wants an article on this trend.
prompts.task: Write an article for a local newspaper explaining the reasons for the rise of the athleisure trend.

prompts.topic: Summer poetry workshop
prompts.context: A summer poetry workshop is being organized by the Hong Kong Arts Development Council. You want to apply for a place.
prompts.task: Write a letter of application explaining why you are interested in participating in the workshop and why you should be selected to take part.

prompts.topic: Open bird cage
prompts.context: Write a short story for a competition.
prompts.task: Write a short story describing the events leading to an open bird cage and the events that take place afterwards.

prompts.topic: Yearbook entry about your school life
prompts.context: You are graduating this year and the school yearbook committee has asked you to write a personal entry about your school life.
prompts.task: Write a yearbook entry describing your most memorable experiences at school, the friends you have made, and your hopes for the future.

prompts.topic: Hiring international talents
prompts.context: Hong Kong companies are increasingly hiring international talent. There is debate about the impact on local workers.
prompts.task: Write an essay explaining some benefits and challenges that international talent brings to Hong Kong and suggesting ways to ensure that people from the local community are not neglected.

prompts.topic: Do social media platforms encourage or stifle public debate?
prompts.context: There is ongoing debate about whether social media platforms encourage meaningful public debate or actually stifle it.
prompts.task: Write an argumentative essay choosing one side of the argument and stating your position with reasons and evidence.

prompts.topic: Unexpected incident during the final game of the season
prompts.context: During the final game of the school sports season, an unexpected incident occurred. You are reporting on it for the school newsletter.
prompts.task: Write a news report describing the incident and what happened afterwards, including thoughts and opinions from different people involved.

prompts.topic: Designing a campaign poster for a district council election candidate
prompts.context: You are working on a political campaign and need to present your design ideas for a campaign poster.
prompts.task: Write an email to the council election committee explaining the ideas behind your designs and how they would make a good campaign theme, and share an idea for a new design.

prompts.topic: New Cinderella film
prompts.context: A new film adaptation of Cinderella has been released. Your school magazine wants a review.
prompts.task: Write a film review including a brief description of the plot, how the characters are portrayed, and why the director wanted to make this film.

prompts.topic: Criticism and how to handle it
prompts.context: You are preparing a speech about the role of criticism in personal growth.
prompts.task: Write a speech discussing what a critic does, how to give criticism, and ways to respond to criticism. Refer to a relevant quote in your speech.
taskRequirements: Need to refer to the quote provided

prompts.topic: The New Tortoise and the Hare
prompts.context: Write a modern version of The Tortoise and the Hare for a short story competition.
prompts.task: Write a short story about a race, include an ending where the two characters team up to race against other contestants and win, and include a message for the story.

prompts.topic: New Cinderella
prompts.context: A new version of Cinderella has been staged at the Hong Kong Cultural Centre.
prompts.task: Write a personal email to a friend recommending the show and describing what was different about this version.

prompts.topic: Restaurant review
prompts.context: You recently visited a new restaurant that opened in your neighbourhood. Your school magazine is featuring student reviews of local eateries.
prompts.task: Write a review of the restaurant describing the food, service, atmosphere, and whether you would recommend it to others.

prompts.topic: Warning labels on foods high in sugar, saturated fat and salt
prompts.context: The government is considering introducing mandatory warning labels on foods high in sugar, saturated fat, and salt.
prompts.task: Write an argumentative essay arguing either for or against the effectiveness of food warning labels in changing people's eating habits.

prompts.topic: Small independent stationery shops in Hong Kong closing
prompts.context: Small independent stationery shops in Hong Kong are closing down due to competition from large chains and online shopping.
prompts.task: Write a news report explaining this trend and discussing whether independent stationery shops can survive.

prompts.topic: Proposing Dragon Boat Racing for future Olympics
prompts.context: The International Olympic Committee is considering new sports for future Olympic Games. You believe Dragon Boat Racing should be included.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the International Olympic Committee explaining how Dragon Boat Racing appeals to young people, promotes gender equality, and attracts media coverage.

prompts.topic: Applying for a work transfer
prompts.context: You would like to transfer to a different department within your company. You need to email your manager to request this.
prompts.task: Write an email to your manager highlighting your work experience, explaining why you would like to transfer, and highlighting how your transfer will benefit the organisation.

prompts.topic: Hong Kong's housing estates becoming Instagram spots
prompts.context: Hong Kong's public housing estates have become popular Instagram spots for photographers and tourists.
prompts.task: Write an essay discussing what attracts Instagrammers to Hong Kong's housing estates and how residents feel about this.

prompts.topic: Best New Artist rising to fame
prompts.context: A new singer called Zoe has won the Best New Artist award. You are writing an article for a music magazine.
prompts.task: Write a feature article for a magazine reporting on Zoe's sudden rise to fame and where the ideas behind her songs came from.

prompts.topic: Disappearing staff in a research station
prompts.context: Write the first chapter of a story set in a research station where staff members are mysteriously disappearing.
prompts.task: Write the first chapter only of a story based on this scenario.

prompts.topic: Watching Chinese Opera at Xiqu Centre
prompts.context: You recently watched a Chinese Opera performance at the new Xiqu Centre in West Kowloon Cultural District.
prompts.task: Write a blog post describing your experience watching Chinese Opera and describing the impact it had on you.

prompts.topic: Speech about environmental protection
prompts.context: Your school is celebrating Green Week. You have been asked to give a speech during morning assembly about environmental protection.
prompts.task: Write a speech encouraging your fellow students to adopt more environmentally friendly habits. Explain why individual actions matter and suggest three simple changes students can make.

prompts.topic: Social media influencers
prompts.context: Social media influencers have become a major force in advertising and marketing.
prompts.task: Write an argumentative essay discussing whether social media influencers are more trustworthy than traditional media advertisements.

prompts.topic: Low sales of electric cars
prompts.context: Sales of electric vehicles in Hong Kong remain low despite government incentives.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor discussing why sales of electric vehicles are so low and suggesting what can be done to attract more people to drive these vehicles.

prompts.topic: Professional athlete looking for a career change
prompts.context: A professional athlete you know is looking for a career change. You are helping them write a job application.
prompts.task: Write a job application letter introducing the athlete and their athletic history, explaining their interest and suitability for the job.

prompts.topic: Cancelling the no-exchange no-refund policy
prompts.context: Your company has a no-exchange, no-refund policy that many customers complain about. Your manager has asked you to review it.
prompts.task: Write an email to the sales manager explaining why the policy should be changed, suggesting what changes could be made, and discussing ways to prevent customers from taking advantage of the new policy.

prompts.topic: The 21-day Gratitude Challenge
prompts.context: You took on a 21-day Gratitude Challenge and wrote about your experiences.
prompts.task: Write a blog post explaining why you accepted this challenge, what you hoped to achieve, and reflecting on the 21 days and whether taking on this challenge has changed you.

prompts.topic: Scary story about a security guard on night duty
prompts.context: Write a scary story based on four pictures: two people in the security room, a security guard falling asleep, an alarm ringing, a security guard patrolling (use in any order).
prompts.task: Write a scary story using these four pictures in any order.
taskRequirements: Use the four pictures provided in any order

prompts.topic: New friends and old friends
prompts.context: You are writing an essay about friendship, inspired by poems and songs you have studied.
prompts.task: Write an essay discussing what is the difference between old and new friendships and whether older friendships are better than new ones.

prompts.topic: New TV drama series about three generations of a family
prompts.context: You have an idea for a new TV drama series about three generations of a Hong Kong family. You need to pitch it to a production company.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the production manager introducing the characters, plot and setting of your drama series, and explaining why the idea would be popular with viewers.

prompts.topic: Guide for visitors to your school
prompts.context: Your school is hosting a delegation of exchange students from overseas. You have been asked to write a short visitor guide to help them navigate the school.
prompts.task: Write a visitor guide describing the school facilities, highlighting important locations, and offering tips for making the most of their stay.

prompts.topic: YouTube channel from a teenage perspective
prompts.context: You run a YouTube channel from a teenage perspective. Teen Magazine wants an article about your experience.
prompts.task: Write an article for Teen Magazine explaining why you started this channel and what you have learned in the process.

prompts.topic: Opening a cafe vs accepting a promotion
prompts.context: A reader has written to a job magazine asking for advice about whether to open their own cafe or accept a promotion at their current company.
prompts.task: Write a letter of advice for a job magazine giving guidance on this career decision.

prompts.topic: Being second in the inter-school championship
prompts.context: Your team came second in the inter-school championship. You have been asked to give a speech at the awards ceremony.
prompts.task: Write a speech reflecting on coming second in the championship, acknowledging the team's efforts and what you have learned from the experience.

prompts.topic: Young people's lack of interest in traditional art forms
prompts.context: There is concern that young people in Hong Kong are losing interest in traditional art forms.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor expressing your views on young people's lack of interest in traditional art forms.

prompts.topic: Bio for a musical group
prompts.context: A new musical group needs an artist biography for their promotional materials.
prompts.task: Write an artist biography including background information about the group, their style of music, and their plans for the future.

prompts.topic: The hero and the villain
prompts.context: You performed in a school play as either the hero or the villain.
prompts.task: Write a journal entry reflecting on which role you preferred playing and explaining the reasons.

prompts.topic: Story about survival in nature
prompts.context: Write a story for a writing competition.
prompts.task: Write a short story about a hiker who got caught in a storm while hiking in the mountains.

prompts.topic: Four-day school week
prompts.context: Your school is considering implementing a four-day school week. The Parent-Teacher Association wants to hear students' views.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the President of the PTA stating your opinion either for or against a four-day school week and give reasons to support your view.

prompts.topic: Applying for a school club membership
prompts.context: Your school is recruiting new members for the Student Union. You need to complete an application form.
prompts.task: Complete the application form sections: Personal details, Why you want to join the Student Union, What skills you can contribute, and Any previous leadership experience.

prompts.topic: Hidden gems in Hong Kong
prompts.context: A travel magazine is featuring an article about hidden gems in Hong Kong that tourists often miss.
prompts.task: Write a travel magazine article recommending lesser-known places in Hong Kong that are worth visiting.

prompts.topic: Impact of social media on teenagers' mental health
prompts.context: Your school magazine is exploring the impact of social media on teenagers' mental health.
prompts.task: Write an article for the school magazine discussing the positive and negative impacts of social media on teenagers' mental health.

prompts.topic: Should Hong Kong introduce a universal basic income?
prompts.context: There is debate about whether Hong Kong should introduce a universal basic income to address poverty and inequality.
prompts.task: Write an essay arguing for or against the introduction of a universal basic income in Hong Kong.

prompts.topic: The future of remote work
prompts.context: Many companies are adopting permanent remote or hybrid working arrangements.
prompts.task: Write an essay discussing the advantages and disadvantages of remote work for employers and employees.

prompts.topic: A song that changed your perspective
prompts.context: You have been studying how songs can influence people's perspectives. Write a diary entry about a song that changed how you see something.
prompts.task: Write a diary entry describing a song that changed your perspective on something important and explain how it affected you.

prompts.topic: Community heroes
prompts.context: A local newspaper is running a series on community heroes who make a difference in their neighbourhoods.
prompts.task: Write a news article profiling a community hero, describing what they do and the impact they have on the community.

prompts.topic: Proposal for a school drama festival
prompts.context: You want to propose a school drama festival to celebrate the performing arts at your school.
prompts.task: Write a formal letter to the Principal proposing a school drama festival, including suggested activities, resource needs, and expected benefits.

prompts.topic: Sports nutrition for young athletes
prompts.context: A restaurant business magazine wants an article about sports nutrition and the dietary needs of young athletes.
prompts.task: Write an article discussing the importance of sports nutrition for young athletes and what restaurants and schools can do to support healthy eating.

prompts.topic: Complaint about a hotel stay
prompts.context: After seeing an advertisement, you booked a one-night stay at the Seaside Hotel. However, you were not happy with your stay because the room was very different from what was shown on the hotel's website.
prompts.task: Write an email to the hotel manager explaining why you were disappointed and what you would like the manager to do.

prompts.topic: Smaller family size
prompts.context: In recent years, there has been a trend towards smaller family sizes in Hong Kong.
prompts.task: Write an essay explaining why having a smaller family is becoming more desirable and discuss the implications.

prompts.topic: Musicians promoting their music
prompts.context: Independent musicians in Hong Kong are finding new ways to promote their music.
prompts.task: Write a blog post discussing how Hong Kong musicians can promote their music effectively in the digital age.

prompts.topic: Dog-friendly public spaces
prompts.context: There has been debate about whether Hong Kong should have more dog-friendly public spaces.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor expressing your views on creating more dog-friendly public spaces in Hong Kong.

prompts.topic: A video that went viral
prompts.context: A video created by your schoolmates has gone viral on social media, attracting both praise and criticism.
prompts.task: Write a school magazine article about the video that went viral, describing its content, why it became popular, and the reactions it received.

prompts.topic: Star Ferry exhibition website article
prompts.context: Hong Kong's Star Ferry, a beloved Icon of the city, is celebrating its 125th anniversary. An exhibition is being organized and your article will be featured on the exhibition website.
prompts.task: Write an article for the exhibition website about the Star Ferry's history, its significance to Hong Kong, and what makes it special to you.

prompts.topic: Using government fund for school improvement
prompts.context: The government has announced that schools can apply for a grant to improve the school environment. Write a proposal to the School Management Committee.
prompts.task: Write a proposal suggesting how the school could use the government grant to improve facilities, enhance learning, or benefit the school community.

prompts.topic: Hong Kong pop culture influenced by Japan and Korea
prompts.context: Hong Kong's pop culture has been significantly influenced by Japanese and Korean trends.
prompts.task: Write a blog post discussing how Japanese and Korean pop culture have influenced Hong Kong's youth culture and entertainment scene.

prompts.topic: Land use for open space vs development
prompts.context: The Town Planning Board is considering converting some residential land into open space. You strongly believe that more land should be used for open space.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the Town Planning Board expressing your views on why more land should be used for open space in Hong Kong.

prompts.topic: Meeting an old classmate at a theme park
prompts.context: You are entering a short story competition.
prompts.task: Write a short story about unexpectedly meeting an old classmate at a theme park.

prompts.topic: People staying long in coffee shops
prompts.context: Some coffee shops in Hong Kong have complained about customers staying for hours without making additional purchases.
prompts.task: Write a letter to the editor expressing your views on this issue and suggesting how coffee shops and customers can reach a reasonable compromise.

prompts.topic: Challenges of group work in the workplace
prompts.context: Group work is common in modern workplaces but many employees find it challenging.
prompts.task: Write an essay discussing the challenges of group work in the workplace and suggesting how these challenges can be overcome.

prompts.topic: Trying vegetarianism for a week
prompts.context: You recently tried being vegetarian for a week and want to share your experience.
prompts.task: Write a blog post describing your experience of trying vegetarianism for a week, including what you learned, what you struggled with, and whether you would recommend it.
